summary: Matched a large additional portion of Samus fighter code by moving functions out of asm/melee/ft/chara/ftsamus.s into new C translation units ftsam3.c, ftsam4.c, and ftsam5.c. The work covers Samus special-move logic including SpecialN, SpecialS, and SpecialHi start/actions, updates Samus action-state table entries to point at newly named ftSamus_* symbols, expands Samus-specific attributes/state typing, and centralizes M_PI in include/math.h. No review discussion was present in the slice.
what_changed: Deleted about 1857 lines from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftsamus.s and added matched C implementations in ftsam3.c, ftsam4.c, and ftsam5.c.

Added ftsam3.c with Samus neutral-special-related code, including ftSamus_80129100, ftSamus_80129158, ftSamus_801291A8, ftSamus_801291F0, ftSamus_80129258, ftSamus_801292E4, ftSamus_801293BC, ftSamus_SpecialN_StartAction, ftSamus_SpecialAirN_StartAction, and many action callbacks.

Added ftsam4.c with Samus side-special-related code, including ftSamus_ClearThrowFlagsUnk, ftSamus_SpecialS_StartAction, ftSamus_SpecialAirS_StartAction, and callbacks in the ftSamus_8012A380 through ftSamus_8012A640 range.
Added ftsam5.c with Samus up-special-related code, including ftSamus_SpecialHi_StartAction, ftSamus_SpecialAirHi_StartAction, ftSamus_DestroyAllUnsetx2444, ftSamus_8012A81C, and ftSamus_8012A8C4.
Updated Samus action-state table entries in remaining assembly from lbl_* callback labels to address-based ftSamus_* function symbols.
Added the three new Samus C object files to obj_files.mk before the remaining ftsamus.s object.
Expanded ftSamusAttributes in ftsamus.h: changed x1C from s32 to f32 and replaced filler around x24-x50 with named f32 fields.
Added Samus helper inlines in ftsamus.h for resetting damage/death callbacks, clearing x2234, destroying effects, and clearing x222C/x2234 effect state.
Added x2348_stateVar3_f32 to Fighter's x2348 union for float-typed Samus state usage.
Promoted M_PI to include/math.h and removed local M_PI defines from ftbosslib.c, ftcommon.c, ground.c, and sysdolphin/baselib/mtx.c.
Updated include/functions.h and asm/melee/it/code_8027CF30.s to refer to ftSamus_* names and added a prototype for func_802B56E4(u32, Vec*, f32, f32, f32).
smart_moves: Kept unmatched residue in ftsamus.s while adding matched C object files in build order, allowing incremental decomp without requiring the whole Samus file to be converted at once.
Renamed action-state table callback targets from generic lbl_* labels to ftSamus_* symbols, making the action table searchable and tying callbacks to the new C implementations.

Factored repeated Samus cleanup/callback patterns into header inline helpers such as ftSamus_updateDamageDeathCBs, ftSamus_destroyAllEF, and ftSamus_UnkAndDestroyAllEF, which likely helped preserve inlining while reducing duplicated C.
Expanded ftSamusAttributes only where the new code required concrete typed fields, replacing broad filler with f32 members x24 through x50.
Centralized M_PI in include/math.h instead of keeping several file-local duplicate defines, reducing future compile conflicts as more C files include math constants.
Recorded matching pain points directly in comments, including unused stack filler arrays and an inline/regalloc note, which gives later decompilers clues about why odd locals exist.
decomp_lessons: For fighter files with large asm coverage, splitting matched functions across multiple thematic C files can make progress easier while preserving the original asm file for remaining unmatched sections.
Action-state table decomp benefits from renaming callback labels early; even address-based ftSamus_* names are more maintainable than lbl_* when cross-referenced by tables and other systems.
Header-level inline helpers are a practical way to match repeated cleanup/callback code when the original compiler likely inlined small routines.
Do not leave broad struct filler when newly matched code proves field types; replacing filler with f32/s32 fields improves readability and helps downstream functions match.
Adding a typed union alias, such as x2348_stateVar3_f32, is preferable to casting when one action state treats an existing state variable as a float.

Unused stack locals may be necessary for matching; this PR documents several explicitly, e.g. comments like 'could not match all the stack without this' and 'must have been something left over from removed code'.
naming_conventions: Matched but semantically unnamed Samus functions use address-based names with the ftSamus_ prefix, e.g. ftSamus_80129100 and ftSamus_8012A8C4.

Known action entry points keep semantic names such as ftSamus_SpecialN_StartAction, ftSamus_SpecialAirN_StartAction, ftSamus_SpecialS_StartAction, ftSamus_SpecialAirS_StartAction, ftSamus_SpecialHi_StartAction, and ftSamus_SpecialAirHi_StartAction.
Samus-specific fighter state fields are referenced under fighter->sa.samus with offset-style names like x222C, x2230, and x2234.
Fighter callback fields use descriptive offset names such as x21DC_callback_OnTakeDamage and x21E4_callback_OnDeath2.
Struct fields in ftSamusAttributes remain offset-based where semantics are unknown, but concrete numeric type names such as f32 x24 through f32 x50 replace anonymous filler when used.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed large matched text and sdata2 constants from ftsamus.s while leaving unmatched labels such as lbl_8012A96C in place.
Changed assembly references in asm/melee/it/code_8027CF30.s from func_80129100-style names to ftSamus_80129100-style names so external branches match the new symbol names.
Used stack filler arrays and unused locals to reproduce compiler stack layout in several functions, based on comments in ftsam3.c/f tsam4.c excerpts.
Used inline helpers in ftsamus.h for repeated small routines likely emitted inline by the compiler.
Added a concrete prototype for func_802B56E4 with Vec* and f32 arguments, allowing C code to call it with typed parameters.
Moved duplicate M_PI constants into include/math.h to avoid local macro duplication as more math-using code becomes C.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename ftSamus_80129100 or other address-based Samus functions to semantic names.
Check whether the inline helpers added to ftsamus.h were later moved into C files or kept as header inlines for matching.
Search for follow-up work on remaining unmatched labels in asm/melee/ft/chara/ftsamus.s after this PR.
Look for later changes refining ftSamusAttributes fields x24 through x50 and height_attributes.
Inspect later PRs for cleanup of unused stack filler comments in ftsam3.c, ftsam4.c, and ftsam5.c.
